symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent crampy abdominal pain related to defecation over a 4-year background, acutely flared by stress

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain related to defecation on a chronic recurrent background is a core Rome IV feature of IBS; stress as a trigger fits the gut-brain mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Altered bowel habit alternating between constipation and diarrhoea with a change in stool form (mixed subtype)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A change in stool frequency and form with alternating constipation and diarrhoea defines the IBS mixed subtype.
Altered bowel habit is compatible with IBD but non-specific; the discriminator is the presence/absence of blood, inflammation and systemic features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Passage of clear mucus per rectum with a sensation of incomplete evacuation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear mucus and a feeling of incomplete evacuation are recognised functional features of IBS; clear (not bloody) mucus argues against inflammatory or malignant causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe visible abdominal distension with colicky cramping, stress-triggered

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stress-provoked colonic spasm trapping luminal gas produces cramps and gaseous distension out of proportion to gas volume in a hypersensitive gut.

Acute severe cramping distension overlaps with obstruction and mandates its active exclusion — reassured here by ongoing passage of stool/flatus and absence of vomiting, but not formally excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative alarm-feature screen — no rectal bleeding/melaena, no weight loss, no nocturnal symptoms, no fever, no vomiting; still passing stool and wind

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of rectal bleeding, nocturnal symptoms, fever and weight loss lowers the probability of IBD, though inflammation is not excluded without bloods/calprotectin.
Ongoing passage of stool and flatus with no vomiting makes obstruction much less likely, though not formally excluded without exam/imaging.
A negative alarm-feature screen and a stable chronic pattern lower malignancy probability but do not remove the need for baseline in-person work-up given the altered bowel habit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, globally tympanic, non-rigid abdomen with only mild diffuse left-lower-quadrant tenderness along the descending colon, active normal-pitched bowel sounds, and no guarding, rebound, rigidity, organomegaly or mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft tympanic abdomen with normal bowel sounds and mild colonic tenderness confirms colonic spasm with gaseous distension — a sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain.
Absence of guarding, rebound and rigidity excludes peritonism, and normal bowel sounds argue against obstruction — but this is precisely the palpation/auscultation the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Baseline screening bloods (FBC, ferritin, CRP, coeliac serology) and faecal calprotectin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal bloods and a normal faecal calprotectin are the outpatient step that formally excludes anaemia, coeliac disease and inflammatory bowel disease before IBS is confirmed — not available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic recurrent pain related to defecation with an alternating bowel habit, mucus and incomplete evacuation, plus a negative alarm-feature screen, is a strong functional (Rome IV) pattern — but confirmation still needs the exam in SYM-006 and the exclusionary bloods in SYM-007, which telehealth cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person abdominal examination (percussion, palpation, auscultation) +/- rectal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important next step: distinguishes gaseous distension from obstruction/mass and excludes peritonism — the exclusion the AI Doctor cannot perform remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline bloods — FBC, ferritin, CRP, coeliac serology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes anaemia/iron deficiency, inflammation and coeliac disease as organic contributors before IBS is confirmed.
Distinguishes functional IBS from inflammatory bowel disease in a patient with chronic altered bowel habit.
Not indicated in the absence of any alarm feature or sign of obstruction/peritonism; reserved for if red-flag escalation triggers occur.
